data.title: Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels signs Ayodhya, Ujjain properties under Zone Connect brand
data.description:
Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Ltd (ASPHL) on Monday, April 20, said it has signed two hotel management agreements for Zone Connect by The Park properties in Ayodhya and Ujjain.

The company said the dual signings are part of its growth strategy focused on strengthening its presence across pilgrimage circuits, catering to travellers seeking design-led hospitality in culturally significant locations.

In Ayodhya, Zone Connect by The Park will feature 56 rooms. The hotel will be located near Ram Janmabhoomi, approximately 4.9 km away, and Maharishi Valmiki International Airport, around 6 km away. The property will include Café C as an all-day dining restaurant, a rooftop restaurant, a plunge swimming pool, gym, and a banquet hall for social gatherings and celebrations.

ALSO READ | Park Hotels aims to triple revenue and expand to 5,800 rooms by 2030

In Ujjain, Zone Connect by The Park will offer 64 rooms. It will be situated approximately 7 km from Ujjain Railway Station and 8 km from Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, with access to Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport. The hotel will feature Café C as an all-day dining restaurant, a plunge swimming pool, along with fitness and wellness facilities.

With these additions, Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Ltd expands its presence in two of India’s major pilgrimage destinations through the Zone Connect by The Park brand.

Vikas Ahluwalia, AVP & National Head – Zone by The Park Hotels, said, "We are delighted to expand our footprint in two of India’s most significant spiritual destinations — Ayodhya and Ujjain. These signings mark an important step in strengthening our presence in the pilgrimage tourism segment. As demand for quality hospitality infrastructure continues to grow in these cities, we aim to offer vibrant, contemporary stays."

ALSO READ | The Park opens its first hotel in Patna with eye on buisness, leisure travellers

Vivek Jaiswal, Owner, Hotel Saroj Sons, added, "We are pleased to partner with Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Limited for Ayodhya. With their strong brand legacy and operational expertise, we are confident of creating a compelling hospitality offering for the growing influx of visitors to the city."

Jyoti Saran Agarwal, Partner at Shubh Aagman Hotels LLP, added, "Our collaboration with ASPHL for Ujjain reflects a shared vision of delivering quality hospitality in one of India’s most important spiritual destinations. We look forward to bringing a contemporary and comfortable stay experience to travellers visiting the city."

ALSO READ | Park Hotels to raise ₹350 Cr from Kolkata residence project with Ambuja Neotia Group collaboration

Shares of Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Ltd ended at ₹120.02, down by ₹2.05, or 1.68%, on the BSE today, April 20.

data.title: Russia sacks agriculture official after cattle cull protests go viral
data.description:
Key points:
  • Farmers protested mass cattle culling, citing disputed disease outbreak explanations
  • USDA report suggested possible foot-and-mouth disease, Russian officials denied this
  • Social media influencers amplified protests, prompting nationwide debate

By Gleb Bryanski

A provincial agriculture official in Siberia was sacked on Monday after protests by farmers against the culling of cattle went viral on the internet, in rare criticism of the authorities that has been acknowledged by the Kremlin.

The Novosibirsk regional governor, Andrei Travnikov, said he had fired the region's agriculture minister, Andrei Shindelov, over his handling of cattle disease.

"For the first time in many decades, we have allowed dangerous animal diseases into the region," Travnikov was quoted as telling local officials on the regional government's Telegram channel.

Novosibirsk farmers had protested in March after police and vets culled thousands of cattle. The protests were among issues brought up in a viral video by a social media influencer who lives abroad, Viktoria Bonya, who said that Russians were being squeezed by corrupt officials and might one day erupt.

Her appeal, which received tens of millions of views, was acknowledged this week by the Kremlin and sparked a nationwide discussion on social media about the state of affairs in wartime Russia.

"They came and killed the cattle. If someone started to speak up, they took them to the police so that the others didn't dare," said Bonya.

The farmers' protests were largely ignored by state media but were extensively covered by popular influencers. In one video that went viral, Svetlana Panina, a farmer from the village of Novoklyuchi, chased Shindelov through the corridors of a local administration building saying she would soon not be able to eat or pay her bills.

The protests died down after some of the activists were briefly detained and the government promised compensation to affected farmers.

The authorities described the culling last month as a means to combat outbreaks of two veterinary diseases: pasteurellosis and rabies, although some biologists cast doubt on whether those diseases would have required such widespread culling.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture's Foreign Agricultural Service in a report published last month cited "local sources and trading contacts" who said "the scale of these measures may indicate an unconfirmed outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease".

The Russian agriculture watchdog agency told Reuters that allegations in the USDA report "were not true."

data.title: HOOD’s Stock Gets Rare Price Target Hike After Wave Of Cuts Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Key points:
  • Mizuho cited the SEC’s recent rule change, which removes the $25,000 minimum threshold, as the reason behind the price target hike.
  • The firm estimated that the rule change could increase trading activity by 3% and add between 1% and 2% to the company’s revenue by 2027. 
  • The price target hike comes after a recent wave of cuts from major banks ahead of Robinhood’s first-quarter earnings, on concerns around weaker crypto prices and softer trading volumes. 

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares fell in pre-market trade on Monday despite getting a price target hike from Mizuho amid broader weakness in the market after tensions between the U.S. and Iran escalated over the weekend.

Mizuho raised its price target on HOOD’s shares to $115 from $105 and kept an ‘Outperform’ rating, according to a note cited by TheFly. The new target is above the consensus average of $101.40, implying a potential upside of more than 26%, more than double the 11% based on the consensus.

HOOD’s stock dropped over 1% in pre-market trade. On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around the company continued to trend in ‘extremely bullish’ territory, accompanied by ‘extremely high’ levels of chatter over the past day.

SEC Rule Change Could Support Trading Activity

Mizuho analyst Dan Dolev cited the Securities and Exchange Commission’s (SEC) rule change last week, which has ended the long-standing $25,000 minimum linked to the pattern day trader rule, as the reason behind the price hike. The rule has been replaced with broker-determined intraday margin requirements.

Mizuho analyst Dan Dolev based his price target hike on the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) ending the long-standing $25,000 minimum tied to the pattern day trader rule, replacing it with broker-determined intraday margin requirements.

The firm said it views the move as "structurally positive" for Robinhood, but added its proprietary survey of 160 traders with less than $25,000 suggests the near-term impact would be modest. It estimated that the change could increase trading activity by around 3%.

Mizuho also noted that Robinhood is “over-indexed to smaller balances,” with about 25% of funded accounts affected. This could translate into a 1% to 2% lift to fiscal 2027 revenue estimates.

Wall Street Turns Cautious On HOOD Stock Ahead Of Q1 Earnings

Mizuho’s price target hike comes amid a broader round of cuts across Wall Street, largely tied to softer trading volumes and weaker crypto-related revenue expectations.

Morgan Stanley cut its price target to $95 from $147 while maintaining an ‘Equal Weight’ rating, citing reduced near-term trading activity and pressure on crypto revenues. Truist Financial also trimmed its target to $100 from $120, keeping a ‘Buy’ rating, citing softer crypto prices and declining trading activity.

Bitcoin (BTC) edged 0.3% lower in the last 24 hours, but held above the $75,000 mark. On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around the apex cryptocurrency continued to trend in ‘bullish’ territory over the past day, accompanied by ‘high’ levels of chatter.

Meanwhile, Bernstein lowered its price target on HOOD's stock to $130 from $160 but kept an ‘Outperform’ rating, pointing to muted trading volumes ahead of earnings. Citizens Financial Group reduced its price target to $155 from $180 while maintaining an ‘Outperform’ rating, part of a broader reset that also affected other crypto-linked firms.

HOOD’s stock has fallen over 20% this year but it has more than doubled in value over the past 12 months.

data.title: Board of Italy's Terna to meet on CEO severance pay, source says
data.description:
Italy's state-controlled power grid operator Terna will hold an extraordinary board meeting in the next few days to discuss the severance pay of its outgoing CEO, a source close to the company told Reuters on Monday.

Giuseppina Di Foggia has been nominated as chair of Eni by the Italian government, but is unwilling to give up a 7.3 million euro ($8.6 million) severance package from Terna.

Terna's board met on Monday, but did not discuss her case, the source said. On Sunday, Italy's economy ministry said it was against state-backed groups paying severance packages to executives whose terms expire or who resign voluntarily.

The issue must be resolved before May 6, when Eni holds its shareholders' meeting. By then, Di Foggia must step down as Terna's chief executive to take up the new role.

The source, who spoke on condition of anonymity because the issue is private, said Di Foggia's contract did not include a clause preventing her from receiving severance pay at the end of the mandate or in case of an early resignation.

The dispute is politically awkward for Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's government, at a time when rising energy costs due to the Iran war weigh on households and firms.

Italy's Treasury owns a 29.85% stake in Terna through state lender CDP. The ministry and CDP also control Eni with a combined 33.1% shareholding.

($1 = 0.8500 euros)

data.title: Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina Q1 net earnings edge up, helped by loan growth
data.description:
Overview

  • US community bank's Q1 net earnings rose slightly from prior year

  • LOWER INTEREST EXPENSE - Decreased rates paid on interest-bearing liabilities reduced interest expense, benefiting net interest income

data.title: Bank of Hawai‘i posts Q1 2026 EPS $1.30; net income $57.4M, NIM 2.74%
data.description:
Bank of Hawai‘i reported first-quarter 2026 diluted earnings per common share of $1.30 and net income of $57.4 million, down 5.7% from the linked quarter. Net interest income improved to $151.0 million and net interest margin rose to 2.74%, supported by lower deposit costs after late‑2025 rate cuts. The company repurchased $15.1 million of common stock and declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.70 per share.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $57.4 million for Q1 2026 (down 5.7% from prior quarter).
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $1.30 for Q1 2026 (basic EPS $1.32).
  • Net interest income: $150.99 million for Q1 2026; net interest margin: 2.74% (up 13 bps sequentially).
  • Total noninterest income: $41.33 million for Q1 2026; total noninterest expense: $116.07 million.
  • Share repurchases: $15.1 million (194.1 thousand shares) in Q1 2026; remaining buyback authority: $105.9 million as of March 31, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Deposit trends: Total deposits were $21.0 billion at March 31, 2026, down 1.1% from December 31, 2025; noninterest-bearing deposits represented 27.0% of total.
  • Deposit pricing: Average rate on interest-bearing deposits decreased to 1.72%; average quarterly total deposit cost (including noninterest-bearing) was 1.26% (down 17 bps sequentially); reported deposit beta for the downward cycle was 36%.
  • Loan portfolio: Total loans and leases were $14.2 billion at quarter end, up 0.8% from December 31, 2025, driven by commercial mortgage production; consumer loans largely stable with slight decline in amortization/paydowns.
  • Asset mix and securities: Investment securities portfolio increased to $7.9 billion, largely U.S. government and agency securities, reflecting purchases of available-for-sale securities.
  • Asset quality and credit: Non-performing assets totaled $12.1 million (0.09% of loans and foreclosed real estate); allowance for credit losses on loans and leases was $147.0 million (1.04% of loans); provision for credit losses was $1.8 million for the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: BANK OF HAWAII CORP [ BOH ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Warsh's AI Optimism May Not Justify Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh has argued that a coming productivity boom may give the Federal Reserve scope to bring interest rates lower, if greater productivity allows for low-inflation economic growth. But though economist Ed Yardeni also anticipates a technological boost to the economy this decade, he disagrees that outcome would justify lower rates. "While we share Warsh's optimism about productivity, we reach a fundamentally different conclusion about what it means for monetary policy," Yardeni writes to clients. Yardeni believes that faster growth will increase the natural rate of interest, or R*, the baseline rate the Fed targets to neither stimulate nor restrain the economy. "If the Fed lowers the federal funds rate below R*, the risk is that it will fuel financial speculation and instability," he writes. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattrossman)

data.title: Records all around, but the Dow RSVP's 'No'
data.description:
Key points:
  • US equity index futures red; Russell 2000 down ~0.7%
  • Euro STOXX 600 index off ~1%
  • Dollar edges up; gold dips; bitcoin rises; US crude rallies >5%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield edges up to ~4.27%

RECORDS ALL AROUND, BUT THE DOW RSVP'S 'NO'

U.S. equity index futures are under pressure heading into Monday's open, with renewed tensions in the Middle East weighing on sentiment. Over the weekend, concerns resurfaced around the Strait of Hormuz, a critical artery for global energy supplies, and that's proving enough to knock markets off balance, at least early on.

That said, the market may have already been due for a breather. On Friday, the Nasdaq Composite pulled off a rare feat, notching its 13th straight daily gain. That kind of streak doesn't come around often — the last time the Nasdaq managed it was back in January 1992. For added historical flavor, the index rose 14 days in a row in June 1987.

Friday's rally was broad, too. A long list of indexes joined the new highs party, including the S&P 500 , the S&P MidCap 400 Index (.MID), the S&P SmallCap 600 Index (.SPCY), the Russell 2000 , the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index , and even the Dow Jones Transportation Average . In other words, just about everyone showed up to the celebration.

Everyone, that is, except the Dow Jones Industrial Average .

Indeed, the market's elder statesman continues to be the odd man out. On Friday, it failed to push through resistance near its February highs, just shy of the 50,000 psychological level.

After reaching an intraday high near 49,718, the index faded and closed at 49,447 — still more than 2% below its February intraday record.

With E-mini Dow futures (YMcv1) down around 200 points ahead of the open, the blue-chip average's failure to confirm the broader market's new highs remains a sticking point for investors, especially those who follow Dow Theory.

Bulls, however, will argue that pullbacks so far have been relatively orderly. Initial support levels loom below, including an April 17 gap near 48,683 and the 50-day moving average around 47,940. Whether this weakness proves shallow could determine if the DJI gets another clean shot at reclaiming its highs.

data.title: Arcus Biosciences Discontinues Lung Cancer Trial With Gilead
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Arcus Biosciences is discontinuing a phase 3 lung cancer trial it was conducting with Gilead Sciences.

Arcus, an oncology company, said Monday it was ending the trial due to futility, meaning it doesn't expect the trial to yield positive results. It also ended a phase 2 study.

Gilead's option rights to certain early pipeline programs under its 2020 agreement with Arcus will end in July due to Gilead's decision to not make the option continuation payment to Arcus.

Gilead won't have option rights to additional Arcus programs, but will maintain its existing time-limited options.

The decision is based on the recommendation of the Independent Data Monitoring Committee, following its review of data from a pre-planned futility analysis.

The analysis didn't assess safety, but no new safety issues were identified during regular reviews.

Gilead is communicating with investigators to determine appropriate next steps for patients in the studies.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Amazon Stock Slips Away From Record New High. AI and 2 Other Reasons It Can Get There. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Amazon.com stock was stalling short of a new high on Monday despite a price-target hike that implies the online retailer could rally 30%.

KeyBanc analyst Justin Patterson hiked his target price to $325 from $285. He said he expects the artificial-intelligence boom to carry on driving growth for the company's cloud-computing business Amazon Web Services, adding that rapid recurring revenue growth for AWS customer Anthropic "provides a meaningful tailwind."

Patterson also touted solid demand for groceries and the looming launch of satellite internet provider Amazon Leo. Amazon said last week that it had agreed to buy satellite company Globalstar, giving it more spectrum.

Amazon slid 1% to $248.01 ahead of Monday's opening bell, sliding as tensions between the U.S. and Iran put a recent market rally in peril. The stock finished Friday at $250.56, just 1.4% short of the record closing high it hit in November 2025.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Consumers Bancorp reports Q3 net income of $2.82M; nine-month net income $8.21M, EPS $2.61
data.description:
Consumers Bancorp reported third-quarter fiscal 2026 net income of $2.82 million, or $0.90 per share, up from $1.85 million a year earlier. For the nine months ended March 31, 2026, net income rose to $8.21 million, or $2.61 per share, driven by a $4.6 million increase in net interest income and a higher net interest margin. Total loans and deposits expanded materially year-to-date, supporting asset redeployment and improved operating metrics.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $2,817 thousand for the three months ended March 31, 2026; $8,206 thousand for the nine months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Earnings per share (basic and diluted): $0.90 for the quarter; $2.61 for the nine months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Net interest income: $10,066 thousand for the quarter; $29,516 thousand for the nine months ended March 31, 2026.
  • Net interest margin (fully tax equivalent): 3.53% for the quarter; 3.45% year-to-date.
  • Total assets: $1,238,168 thousand as of March 31, 2026; total loans: $904,057 thousand; total deposits: $1,109,884 thousand.

Business Highlights

  • Loan growth: Total loans increased by $90.6 million from June 30, 2025 to March 31, 2026; year-to-date originations totaled $297.5 million in new commitments and draws, with $122.6 million of originations compared with the prior-year nine-month period.
  • Undisbursed construction commitments: Undisbursed commitments on residential and commercial construction projects rose by $10.2 million during the quarter to $49.1 million, reserved for in the provision for credit losses at origination.
  • Deposit growth and branch contribution: Total deposits increased by $73.1 million since June 30, 2025, with new business from recently opened branches contributing to the growth.
  • Asset quality and credit performance: Non-performing loans were $547 thousand (including $140 thousand SBA-guaranteed) and non-performing loans to total loans were 0.06% as of March 31, 2026; net charge-offs were $69 thousand for the quarter and $257 thousand year-to-date (annualized 0.04% of total loans).
  • Operational drivers of fee income and expenses: Increases in debit/credit card interchange, mortgage banking revenue, bank-owned life insurance income, and interest rate swap fees supported an 11.2% rise in noninterest income, while staffing additions and technology/security investments drove higher salaries, occupancy and software expenses.

Original SEC Filing: CONSUMERS BANCORP INC /OH/ [ CBKM ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Aspire Biopharma closes $21M private placement, secures $22.5M credit commitment for DCS buy
data.description:
Aspire Biopharma closed the second tranche of a private placement raising $21.0 million and secured a commitment letter for up to $22.5 million to finance the proposed acquisition of Dura Control Systems (DCS).

Key Highlights:

  • Closed final tranche: issued 12,500 Series A Preferred Shares for $10.0M, bringing total gross proceeds to $21.0M.
  • Proceeds use: support working capital, fund portion of cash component for proposed DCS acquisition, and general corporate purposes.
  • Nasdaq compliance: proceeds raise stockholders' equity above the $2.5M minimum to maintain Nasdaq Capital Market listing.
  • LOI to acquire DCS: planned $30M all-cash purchase of DCS, which reported $200M+ revenue and $20M+ adjusted EBITDA in FY2025 (unaudited).
  • Credit commitment: entered commitment letter for senior secured credit facility up to $22.5M (5-year term, SOFR + 325bps) to finance DCS; final terms subject to definitive docs.

Original SEC Filing: Aspire Biopharma Holdings, Inc. [ ASBP ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026
